title: drosera longifolia subsp. obovata
intro: Drosera longifolia subsp. obovata, also known as the long-leaved sundew, is a carnivorous plant species that belongs to the Drosera genus, characterized by its ability to capture and digest insects for nutrients. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Drosera longifolia subsp. obovata are said to include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as potential benefits for digestive problems and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often harvested and dried to be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
